title: Sesamum papillosum
taxonomy: Sesamum papillosum is classified within the family Pedaliaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sesame family. This classification is part of the order Lamiales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. Sesamum papillosum is further classified as a genus within the family Pedaliaceae, alongside other closely related species. The exact classification of Sesamum papillosum may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Pedaliaceae family. Further research may be necessary to determine the most up-to-date classification.

intro: Sesamum papillosum, also known as the hairy sesame plant, is an annual plant species belonging to the genus Sesamum, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities that contribute to its potential health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, reducing oxidative stress, and protecting against certain infections. The bioactive constituents of Sesamum papillosum include sesquiterpenes, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare extracts, teas, and ointments, as well as to make infusions for topical applications, such as treating skin conditions and wounds.
